title: epipactis albensis subsp. mecsekensis
intro: Epipactis albensis subsp. mecsekensis, also known as Mecsek helleborine, is a rare, terrestrial orchid native to Europe, specifically found in the Mecsek Mountains of Hungary. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and expectorant properties, which make it useful in treating respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. The health benefits of Mecsek helleborine include its ability to soothe sore throats, reduce fever, and alleviate congestion, making it a popular choice in herbal remedies. The plant can be prepared into various herbal products, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
